question.content: At locations Jia and Yi, infer two blanks from the noon solar-image projections at equinoxes and solstices: (1) Jia relative direction from Yi; (2) annual-curve index of Delta_h. Answer format requirement: <dir>&<curve_index>, where dir is one of {southeast,northeast,northwest,southwest} and curve_index is one of {1,2,3,4}. Strictly forbidden: any extra text beyond this format.

step_1.content: Identify hemisphere positions of Jia and Yi from the side of solar-image offsets across the four dates.
step_1.key_point: Extract the key constraints before committing to the final choice.

step_3.content: Use equinox constraints to force Delta h to be zero at two annual points.
step_3.key_point: Incorrect curves with nonzero equinox gap must be removed.

step_5.content: Combine winter-solstice terminator orientation with equal UT sunset condition.
step_5.key_point: The geometric relation gives Jia southeast of Yi.

step_7.content: Check symmetric solstice peaks and pick the matching option curve.
step_7.key_point: Final pair is determined by consistency of both subquestions.
